summary: The public marketing site is live on roofingrecyclers.com with core stakeholder pages, Supabase-backed lead capture, centralized content, and an expanded RAS/capital-partner narrative. Recent work focused on rebuilding the home-page hero visual, activating Yahoo SMTP lead notification emails, updating the sender/subject branding to read 'Roofing Recyclers Contact Form', proving via live lead-row audit notes that Yahoo rejects the external replyTo pattern with '550 Request failed; Mailbox unavailable', and then deploying the no-replyTo fix so a fresh production lead row recorded notification=sent with a Yahoo message ID.

active_workstreams.name: Marketing site polish
active_workstreams.notes: Home page and high-visibility sections are being refined to meet the intended investor-grade standard.

active_workstreams.name: Investor / capital partner narrative
active_workstreams.notes: Capital Partners page now includes RAS, business-plan, regulatory, and relationship framing.

active_workstreams.name: Lead capture and infrastructure
active_workstreams.notes: Supabase-backed form flow is implemented and deployed. Notification audit logging now runs in production via inbound_leads.notes, the Yahoo replyTo incompatibility was fixed by removing replyTo, and a fresh live submission recorded notification=sent.

active_workstreams.name: Project memory system
active_workstreams.notes: Initialized in docs/project-memory and versioned in git on 2026-03-24.

current_priorities: Use docs/project-memory as the first-read source of truth in future sessions.
Validate the rebuilt home-page hero and continue visual QA on other high-visibility sections.
Keep monitoring real contact-form submissions now that the Yahoo-compatible no-replyTo fix is live and verified.
Plan the next investor-funnel and authority-content features.
risks: Visual quality still depends on human review across breakpoints; the rebuilt home-page hero still needs user validation after deployment.
Some future-facing metrics and impact statements are still placeholders until live data is connected.
Public-facing policy and relationship copy must remain careful to avoid overclaiming.
Yahoo SMTP is working with the current message shape, but the mailbox setup is sensitive and will fail again if external replyTo is reintroduced.
Vercel CLI deploys were unstable during this debugging batch and one attempt returned 'Invalid JSON response' after a long run.
assumptions: docs/project-memory is the canonical durable memory location for this repo.
roofingrecyclers.com remains the preferred canonical host, with www redirected.
The user wants this repo to remain clearly separate from unrelated ventures.
known_issues: Standalone npm run typecheck can fail if executed before a build because tsconfig includes .next/types output; run build first or rerun typecheck after build.
The rebuilt home-page hero passed build and typecheck locally, but the user still needs to validate the visual result in production.
Lead notifications are best-effort by design, so the lead insert remains the primary success path even if email ever fails again.
Previous non-app-password Yahoo credentials failed auth before the working app password was installed.
Yahoo rejects the contact-form notification when the message includes the external replyTo header, but the deployed no-replyTo fix now avoids that failure.
A local SMTP matrix proved that simple and named-sender Yahoo notifications succeed, while adding the external replyTo causes the failure.
Notification audit logging now runs in production through inbound_leads.notes and recorded a fresh notification=sent result after the fix.

recent_changes.summary: Added RAS and capital partner content, deck-based photos, and stronger investor/business-plan framing.
Aligned site metadata and schema descriptions with RAS positioning.
Initialized docs/project-memory and recorded durable project state, decisions, tasks, file index, and knowledge.
Committed and pushed the project memory system so durable knowledge is versioned in the repo and remote backup.

Rebuilt the home hero visual into a simpler full-width media block, thesis cards, and stacked operating sequence after the user flagged the prior text layout as unacceptable; local build and typecheck both passed.
Committed and pushed the hero rebuild batch to origin/main so the live site can redeploy from GitHub.

Added optional SMTP-based lead notification emails that trigger after the existing Supabase lead insert, defaulting the recipient to warephoto@yahoo.com when mailer configuration is present; build and typecheck passed.
Committed and pushed the lead-notification batch to origin/main so Vercel can redeploy the updated form pipeline.
Prefilled local Yahoo SMTP settings in .env.local for lead notifications, leaving only SMTP_PASSWORD blank pending the user's Yahoo app password.

Tested a supplied Yahoo SMTP credential, confirmed Yahoo auth failure (535 / AUTH005), removed SMTP_PASSWORD from local and Vercel production to disable bad retries, redeployed production, and deleted the exact SMTP test lead row created during verification.
Verified a newly supplied Yahoo password directly against SMTP before changing config and received the same Yahoo auth failure (535 / AUTH005), so no local or Vercel SMTP_PASSWORD update was applied.

Verified a Yahoo app password successfully, restored SMTP_PASSWORD locally and in Vercel production, redeployed the live site, sent a direct SMTP test email successfully, confirmed the production lead endpoint succeeded with SMTP active, and deleted the exact test lead row afterward.

Updated the notification sender/subject branding to 'Roofing Recyclers Contact Form', redeployed production, confirmed the user's latest live submission reached Supabase, and verified that the current formatted SMTP send is still accepted by Yahoo even though the user reported not seeing the notification email.

Added local notification audit logging to inbound_leads notes, proved via a live production test row that Yahoo rejects the external replyTo header with '550 Request failed; Mailbox unavailable', and implemented a local mailer fix that removes replyTo.

Deployed the no-replyTo mailer fix to production, verified a fresh live lead row recorded notification=sent with a Yahoo message ID and recipient, and deleted the exact notification audit test rows from inbound_leads.

next_steps: Validate the rebuilt live home-page hero and overall responsive layout with the user after deployment finishes.
Monitor the next real contact-form submissions and confirm they continue recording notification=sent in inbound_leads.notes.
Decide whether the next feature batch should focus on investor funnel improvements, authority content, or data/impact tooling.
Keep project memory updated at the end of each future session and commit memory changes when appropriate.
